Requirement: The reference architecture of NLP4RE tools shall prescribe a common architecture consisting of standalone, reusable modules with a black-box interface.
Rationale: A modular architecture with standalone, reusable components and black-box interfaces promotes maintainability, interoperability, and reuse across NLP4RE tools.

Requirement: The reference architecture shall contain an input module that consumes software artifacts in various data formats and produces an output for a pre-processing module.
Rationale: An input module capable of handling diverse data formats enables flexibility and integration with multiple RE data sources.

Requirement: The reference architecture shall contain a pre-processing module that consumes the output of an input module, pre-processes the input software artifacts, and produces an output for a processing module.
Rationale: A pre-processing module standardizes and cleans input artifacts, ensuring that subsequent processing stages operate on consistent, structured data.
Comment: The reference architecture shall contain a [MODULE NAME] module that consumes the output of an [INPUT MODULE], [ACTION] the [INPUT MODULE], and produces an output for a [OUTPUT MODULE].

Requirement: The reference architecture shall contain a processing module that consumes the output of a pre-processing module, processes the pre-processed input, and produces an output for a post-processing module.
Rationale: A dedicated processing module encapsulates the core NLP functionality, enabling clear separation of concerns and focused algorithmic development.

Requirement: The reference architecture shall contain a post-processing module that consumes the output of a processing module, post-processes the processed input, and produces an output for an output module.
Rationale: A post-processing module transforms and refines processed outputs to ensure they are interpretable and suitable for presentation or evaluation.

Requirement: The post-processing module shall provide an interface to a evaluation system.
Rationale: Providing an evaluation interface supports benchmarking, comparability, and reproducibility of tool performance.

Requirement: The reference architecture shall contain a output module that consumes the output of a post-processing module, formats this artifact, and visualizes them in a user interface.
Rationale: An output module that formats and visualizes results improves usability and facilitates interpretation by end users.

Requirement: Every module in the RA should preserve every execution (consisting of the input, output, and all auxiliary information) in a secure manner.
Rationale: Securely storing all executions, including inputs, outputs, and metadata, ensures data integrity, reproducibility, and compliance with privacy requirements.

Requirement: The reference architecture of NLP4RE tools shall enforce that every high-level component of a tool explains the syntax and limitations to its input (including the language of NL-based artifacts) and output (including bias of generative models).
Rationale: Explicitly documenting input and output syntax, limitations, and potential biases enhances transparency, trust, and responsible model use.

Requirement: The reference architecture of NLP4RE tools shall enforce that both the overall tool and all of its high-level components explain the output and its relationship to the input.
Rationale: Linking each output to its corresponding input improves interpretability, validation, and traceability throughout RE analyses.

Requirement: The reference architecture of NLP4RE tools should facilitate the consumption and production of different data formats of input and output artifact.
Rationale: Supporting multiple input and output data formats ensures interoperability and integration with diverse tools and workflows.

Requirement: The RA should enforce that the accepted language of the input artifacts is a configurable parameter.
Rationale: Configurable language parameters enable multilingual applicability and adaptability across RE contexts.

Requirement: The RA should enforce that any tool is documented according to a common standard.
Rationale: Standardized documentation ensures consistent understanding, maintenance, and evolution of NLP4RE tools.

Requirement: The documentation of a tool should uniquely identify its version and the version of all the modules it uses.
Rationale: Including version identifiers for both the tool and its modules supports reproducibility and controlled evolution.
Comment: Make clear that this is only one important aspect

Requirement: The documentation of a tool should specify a unique name and contain a comprehensive tool description covering the NLP techniques, NLP task, and RE task, as well as the input and output artifacts it processes.
Rationale: Comprehensive documentation describing the tool’s name, purpose, NLP and RE tasks, and processed artifacts ensures clarity and comparability across implementations.
Domain Concepts: Tool; Documentation; Name; Description; NLP Technology; NLP Task; RE Task

Requirement: The documentation of a tool or module specifies all dependencies and their versions.
Rationale: Listing all dependencies and their versions prevents incompatibilities and facilitates long-term maintainability.

Requirement: The reference architecture shall encourage that all source material of a tool are available long-term online and under an open-source license.
Rationale: Releasing source materials online under open-source licenses ensures accessibility, long-term preservation, and collaborative advancement of NLP4RE tools.
